q: Did you learn to draw or paint when you were younger?
examiner_note: Nói về trải nghiệm học tập thời thơ ấu. Sử dụng 'still-life', 'landscapes', 'perspective and shading', 'nurture'.

band7.text: Yes, I took some basic drawing classes in primary school. We mostly focused on simple still-life drawings and landscapes, which helped me develop a basic understanding of perspective and shading. It was a fun way to spend my time.
band7.note: Sử dụng thuật ngữ nghệ thuật cơ bản (still-life, perspective, shading).

band8.text: I did. My parents encouraged me to take painting classes as a creative outlet. I vividly remember experimenting with watercolors and learning how to blend colors to create different moods. It was a wonderful way to nurture my imagination and develop a fine eye for detail.
band8.note: Sử dụng từ vựng tự nhiên (creative outlet, vividly, watercolors, blend, nurture, fine eye for detail).

band9.text: My childhood was punctuated by various artistic pursuits. I attended an after-school art club where I was introduced to a range of mediums, from charcoal to oil paints. Those early lessons were instrumental in fostering my aesthetic appreciation and taught me to observe the world with much greater precision.
band9.note: Sử dụng từ vựng ở mức độ cao (punctuated, pursuits, mediums, charcoal, instrumental, fostering, aesthetic appreciation, precision).

v.example: I vividly remember the first time I went to the beach.
Oil is a difficult medium to work with.
The work was done with great precision.

advanced_vocab.example: She was instrumental in the project's success.

similar_questions: Do you think drawing is important for children?
What was your favorite subject in primary school?
